title: armeria arenaria subsp. arenaria
intro: Armeria arenaria subsp. arenaria, also known as Sea Pink or Thrift, is a low-growing, perennial flowering plant native to coastal regions of Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Sea Pink is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
